description_long: This object is a vintage cathode-ray tube (CRT) television with a convex glass screen and a thick, dark-colored plastic or metal casing. The front features a large, rounded display bordered by a thin, metallic bezel, with a control panel on the right side that includes several rotary dials and push buttons. Two long, thin metal antennae extend upward from the top, giving it a classic retro appearance. The back of the television is bulbous, accommodating the CRT, and is finished in a textured, dark material. The unit stands on four slender, curved metal legs, elevating it slightly above the ground. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century home electronics, with a utilitarian yet stylish aesthetic.
description: A vintage CRT television with a convex glass screen, metallic bezel, side control panel, and long antennae, standing on four curved legs.
description_view_0: Side view showing the deep, rounded back of the television and the curved legs, with the antennae extending upward.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the convex glass screen, metallic bezel, and control panel with dials and buttons on the right.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, highlighting the depth of the CRT housing and the supporting legs.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the bulbous back of the CRT, textured casing, and the attachment point of the antennae.
attribute_bezel_design: Thin metallic bezel, silver color, simple rectangular styling
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of dials and buttons on the front right panel
